Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your fleet system is accessible to drivers and managers anywhere, protects sensitive data under Singapore's PDPA rules, and connects seamlessly with your accounting and HR systems. SMEs need this foundation to keep operations running smoothly across devices and to comply with data protection regulations. For example, a delivery manager can check vehicle status on their phone while a driver logs trip details on a tablet, and all data syncs securely to your accounting system.

Vehicle Registry.narrative: This family maintains a single source of truth for every vehicle you own or lease, tracks insurance coverage and claims, and manages the full lifecycle from purchase to disposal. SMEs benefit by knowing exactly what assets they have, reducing insurance gaps, and making smarter decisions about vehicle replacement. For instance, when a vehicle is due for renewal, the system flags it automatically, and when an accident occurs, all insurance details are instantly available for the claims process.

Driver & Safety.narrative: This family keeps detailed records of your drivers, monitors unsafe behaviours like speeding or harsh braking, and documents accidents to support insurance claims and safety improvements. SMEs care because safer drivers reduce accidents, lower insurance premiums, and protect your reputation. A driver who consistently scores high on safety metrics becomes a trusted asset, while behaviour alerts let you intervene early—for example, flagging a driver who frequently speeds so you can provide coaching before an incident occurs.

Maintenance & Workshop.narrative: This family automates service reminders based on time or mileage, tracks repair work orders, and ensures vehicles stay roadworthy and compliant. SMEs need this to avoid costly breakdowns, extend vehicle life, and maintain safety standards. When a vehicle hits 40,000 km, the system automatically schedules a service; when a repair is needed, the work order tracks progress and costs, so you always know what's being done and why.

Operations & Logistics.narrative: This family tracks fuel consumption, vehicle location in real time, optimises delivery routes, and manages shared vehicle bookings—turning raw operational data into efficiency gains. SMEs rely on this to cut fuel costs, improve on-time delivery, and maximise vehicle utilisation. A logistics manager can see that a driver is idling in traffic, reroute them to avoid congestion, and a sales team can self-book a pool car for a client visit without calling the office.

Cost & Compliance.narrative: This family calculates the true cost of ownership for each vehicle, generates reports for decision-making, and ensures you meet Singapore's road tax, COE, inspection, and driver-hours regulations. SMEs need this to control costs, demonstrate compliance to authorities, and make data-driven fleet decisions. For example, a dashboard shows that Vehicle A costs 30% more per km than Vehicle B, prompting a review; meanwhile, automated alerts ensure your COE renewal never lapses.

Tyre Management.narrative: This family tracks tyre inventory, monitors tread depth, and schedules rotations to maximise tyre life and safety. While useful for larger fleets, SMEs can often manage tyres through their maintenance work-order process without a dedicated module.

Vendor & Workshop Management.narrative: This family maintains a register of external service providers, tracks their quality and turnaround times, and manages costs. SMEs with small fleets may rely on one or two trusted workshops and can track this informally; larger SMEs benefit from formalising vendor performance.

Environmental & Emissions Tracking.narrative: This family monitors fleet carbon emissions and tracks progress toward EV adoption and sustainability goals. While increasingly important for corporate responsibility, most SMEs do not yet require this unless they have explicit sustainability commitments or customer mandates.

Fleet Analytics & AI.narrative: This family applies machine learning to predict maintenance needs, optimise replacement cycles, and forecast costs. Advanced analytics are valuable for large, complex fleets but are not essential for typical SMEs starting out with fleet management.
